steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two seaweeds. That means the correct choice must contrast sugar kelp and ma-konbu — specifically their umami intensities (moderate vs. high).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Some types of brown seaweed, like sugar kelp and ma-konbu, trigger umami flavor in human taste buds.: Groups both seaweeds together as umami-triggering — no difference shown. ✗
  • Choice B — While sugar kelp and ma-konbu contain umami flavor, umami can also be found in tuna and soy sauce.: Shifts focus to other umami foods; never contrasts the two seaweeds. ✗
  • Choice C — Although sugar kelp and ma-konbu are types of brown seaweed, the latter's umami flavor is more intense.: Directly contrasts the two — sugar kelp moderate, ma-konbu more intense. ✓
  • Choice D — Sugar kelp is a type of brown seaweed, but so is ma-konbu.: Only notes they share a category; says nothing about umami intensity. ✗
That is the correct answer.
